CONTENT.Honours: Dynamo KyivUkrainian Premier League: 2014–15, 2015–16, 2020–21Ukrainian Cup: 2013–14, 2014–15, 2019–20, 2020–21Ukrainian Super Cup: 2016, 2018, 2019, 2020Metalurh ZaporizhzhiaUkrainian First League runner-up: 2011–12
CONTENT.Club career: Serhiy was born in Dzhankoi, Crimean ASSR, Ukrainian SSR but moved at the age of one with his parents to Zaporizhzhia. Sydorchuk is a product of Metalurh Zaporizhzhia academy. He made his debut for Metalurh Zaporizhzhia entering as a second-half substitute against Metalurh Donetsk on 23 August 2009 in the Ukrainian Premier League.On 21 December 2012, Sydorchuk signed a five-year contract with Dynamo. On 11 August 2013, he scored his first goal for the club in a league match against FC Chornomorets Odesa. On 29 September 2015, Sydorchuk made his UEFA Champions League debut in the second match of the group stage against Israeli club Maccabi Tel Aviv away from home, which Dynamo won 2–0.On 5 September 2023, Belgian Pro League club Westerlo announced the signing of Sydorchuk on a three-year contract.

CONTENT.Career statistics: As of match played 18 May 2024As of match played 21 June 2024As of match played 19 June 2023Scores and results list Ukraine's goal tally first, score column indicates score after each Sydorchuk goal.
CONTENT.International career: Sydorchuk made his full international debut for Ukraine on 9 October 2014, replacing captain Ruslan Rotan in the 64th minute of a European qualifier away to Belarus in Barysaw. In added time, he scored the second goal of a 2–0 win, a close-range finish set up by Andriy Yarmolenko. Three days later in the next qualifier at the Arena Lviv, he scored the only goal to defeat Macedonia.

SUMMARY: Serhiy Oleksandrovych Sydorchuk (Ukrainian: Сергій Олександрович Сидорчук; born 2 May 1991) is a Ukrainian professional footballer who plays as a midfielder for Belgian Pro League club Westerlo and the Ukraine national team.
